I agree with the selection of option A as the correct answer. An unpaired (independent samples) t-test is indeed commonly chosen for comparing means between two unrelated groups like students from different classes, especially if their distributions can reasonably be assumed to approximate a normal curve. This aligns well with standard practices in biostatistics where this scenario would arise frequently.

Therefore:
